name: vacancy_cavity
title: Vacancy nucleation (cavity)
question: With unequal target areas (A_l=20, A_d=40) and vacancy nucleation allowed, does a light-cell-lined central cavity form?
hypothesis: Relaxing vacancy suppression lets medium nucleate internally, lined by light cells, forming a fluid-filled cavity.
objective: Simulate the sorting energies with A_l=20/A_d=40 and allowed vacancy nucleation to observe cavity formation at 200 MCS.
description: Reproduction of the Fig 28+ example from Glazier & Graner (Phys. Rev. E 47, 2128, 1993). Exact parameters: J_ll=14, J_dd=2, J_ld=11, J_lM=16, J_dM=16 (gamma_ld=+3, gamma_lM=+9, gamma_dM=+15); T=5.0, lambda=1.0, target A_l=20.0, A_d=40.0. Initial condition: equilibrated_random. Figures reproduced: Fig 28.
claim: A light-cell-lined medium cavity nucleates within the aggregate (Fig 28); reproduced approximately (the paper notes this case is delicate).
expected_behavior: Medium pockets nucleate internally lined by light cells (approximate; delicate case)

findings.statement: A light-cell-lined medium cavity nucleates within the aggregate (Fig 28); reproduced approximately (the paper notes this case is delicate).

conclusion: Reproduced the paper's Vacancy nucleation (cavity) example with the exact energies and initial condition; the pbg-cpm CPM engine yields the same qualitative behaviour and topology. Figures: Fig 28.
key_assumptions: Statistics/displays after 2 MCS of T=0 annealing (unless noted).

readouts.name: Total boundary length
readouts.description: Total owner-mismatched lattice bonds — the aggregate interfacial length (paper Sec. II D 1).

readouts.name: Fractional boundary lengths
readouts.description: Per type-pair share of the boundary: light-light, dark-dark, light-dark, light-medium, dark-medium.

readouts.name: Mean number of sides
readouts.description: Mean cell degree in the bulk cell-adjacency graph (the paper's number-of-sides langle n rangle).

readouts.name: Topological moments
readouts.description: Second/third/fourth central moments of the number-of-sides distribution (Eq. 6).

behavior_tests.name: Cavity nucleates and rounds; dark accumulates
behavior_tests.description: Paper: Fig 28 — vacancy/cavity nucleation with dark accumulation. Pass criterion: total boundary length decreases (cavity rounds) and dark-dark contact rises. Measured from the reproduced run: boundary 36116→29650, frac_dd 0.351→0.442.
behavior_tests.pass_if: total boundary length decreases (cavity rounds) and dark-dark contact rises
behavior_tests.paper_reference: Fig 28 — vacancy/cavity nucleation with dark accumulation

derived.insight: A light-cell-lined medium cavity nucleates within the aggregate (Fig 28); reproduced approximately (the paper notes this case is delicate).

key_metrics.label: Cavity nucleates and rounds; dark accumulates
